summary: The hexagonal split is solid, but there are blocking correctness issues in production rendering and Discord feedback capture that can silently corrupt RLHF data.

issues.description: Production renderer hardcodes a 7-day window string instead of deriving the displayed window from digest data, which can fabricate incorrect time context when backend windows change.
issues.explanation: This keeps the UI truthful to the actual digest window while preserving a safe fallback when legacy payloads do not carry a window field.

issues.description: Capture path has no 429 retry handling and treats non-OK responses as empty arrays, which silently drops scores/whys and can poison preference data.
issues.explanation: Retrying 429 with retry_after preserves correctness under normal Discord throttling and prevents silent feedback loss from transient rate limits.

issues.description: Reply capture only scans one page (`limit=100`) of channel messages, so whys are missed in active channels once messages roll off the first page.
issues.explanation: Paginating message history prevents false negatives for operator rationale capture and keeps attribution reliable when the channel has moderate traffic.

blocking_issues.issue: No robust 429 handling in feedback capture endpoints.
blocking_issues.why_blocking: Ratings and whys can be silently dropped, yielding incorrect RLHF records and bad breeding decisions.
blocking_issues.fix: Use a shared REST helper with bounded retries on 429 using `retry_after`, and route `/users/@me`, `/messages`, and `/reactions` reads through it.

blocking_issues.issue: Hardcoded `last 7 days` in production renderer output.
blocking_issues.why_blocking: Displayed time window can diverge from real digest window, creating fabricated presentation data.
blocking_issues.fix: Derive `windowDays` from `ZoneDigest.raw_stats` (with validated fallback) and interpolate it in the hero text.

concerns: Production renderer hardcodes a time-window string (`last 7 days`) instead of always binding display text to incoming digest data.
